            for block in content:
                if not isinstance(block, dict) or block.get("type") != "tool_use":
                    continue

                tool_name = block.get("name", "")
                tool_id = block.get("id", "")
                tool_input = block.get("input", {})

                if tool_name == "Bash":
                    cmd = tool_input.get("command", "")
                    if "openstation status" in cmd:
                        tool_uses[tool_id] = (line_num, "openstation_status", cmd)
                        add_finding(
                            "CLI_STATUS_INVOCATION", logfile, line_num,
                            "openstation status command: " + cmd
                        )
                        m = re.search(r"openstation\s+status\s+(\S+)\s+(\S+)", cmd)
                        if m:
                            task_ref = m.group(1)
                            new_status = m.group(2).strip("'\"")
                            add_finding(
                                "CLI_STATUS_TRANSITION", logfile, line_num,
                                "Transition attempt: task=" + task_ref + " -> " + new_status,
                                context=cmd
                            )

                if tool_name == "Edit":
                    old_str = tool_input.get("old_string", "")
                    new_str = tool_input.get("new_string", "")
                    file_path = tool_input.get("file_path", "")

                    old_status_match = re.search(r"status:\s*(\S+)", old_str)
                    new_status_match = re.search(r"status:\s*(\S+)", new_str)

                    if old_status_match and new_status_match:
                        old_s = old_status_match.group(1).strip("'\"")
                        new_s = new_status_match.group(1).strip("'\"")
                        if old_s != new_s:
                            is_allowed = (old_s, new_s) in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS
                            category = "FRONTMATTER_STATUS_EDIT"
                            if not is_allowed:
                                category = "FRONTMATTER_INVALID_TRANSITION"
                            add_finding(
                                category, logfile, line_num,
                                "Direct frontmatter edit: " + old_s + " -> " + new_s +
                                " (allowed=" + str(is_allowed) + ") in " + file_path,
                                context="old: " + old_str[:150] + " | new: " + new_str[:150]
                            )
                            if old_s == "review" and new_s == "in-progress":
                                add_finding(
                                    "REWORK_TRANSITION", logfile, line_num,
                                    "Rework: review -> in-progress via direct edit in " + file_path
                                )

                if tool_name == "Write":
                    content_str = tool_input.get("content", "")
                    file_path = tool_input.get("file_path", "")
                    if "status:" in content_str and (
                        "artifacts/tasks/" in file_path or ".openstation/" in file_path
                    ):
                        status_match = re.search(r"status:\s*(\S+)", content_str)
                        if status_match:
                            add_finding(
                                "WRITE_WITH_STATUS", logfile, line_num,
                                "Full file write with status=" +
                                status_match.group(1) + " to " + file_path
                            )

        if msg_type == "result":
            tool_use_id = obj.get("tool_use_id", "")
            result_content = obj.get("result", "")
            if isinstance(result_content, list):
                result_text = " ".join(
                    str(r.get("text", "")) if isinstance(r, dict) else str(r)
                    for r in result_content
                )
            elif isinstance(result_content, dict):
                result_text = str(result_content.get(
                    "text", result_content.get("output", str(result_content))
                ))
            else:
                result_text = str(result_content)

            if tool_use_id in tool_uses:
                orig_line, tool_type, orig_cmd = tool_uses[tool_use_id]
                if tool_type == "openstation_status":
                    err_kws = ["error", "invalid", "fail", "not allowed", "cannot"]
                    if any(kw in result_text.lower() for kw in err_kws):
                        add_finding(
                            "CLI_STATUS_ERROR", logfile, line_num,
                            "Status command failed: " + result_text[:400],
                            context="Original command (line " +
                            str(orig_line) + "): " + orig_cmd
                        )
                    else:
                        add_finding(
                            "CLI_STATUS_SUCCESS", logfile, line_num,
                            "Status command result: " + result_text[:400],
                            context="Original command (line " +
                            str(orig_line) + "): " + orig_cmd
                        )

            if re.search(
                r"(?:invalid|illegal|not\s+allowed)\s+transition",
                result_text, re.IGNORECASE
            ):
                add_finding(
                    "TRANSITION_ERROR_IN_RESULT", logfile, line_num,
                    "Transition error in tool result: " + result_text[:400]
                )


log_files = sorted(LOGS_DIR.glob("*.jsonl"))
print("Analyzing", len(log_files), "log files...")
print()
for lf in log_files:
    print("  Processing:", lf.name)
    analyze_file(lf)

print()
print("=" * 80)
print("TOTAL FINDINGS:", len(findings))
print("=" * 80)
print()

categories = {}
for f in findings:
    cat = f["category"]
    if cat not in categories:
        categories[cat] = []
    categories[cat].append(f)

for cat in sorted(categories.keys()):
    items = categories[cat]
    print()
    print("=" * 80)
    print("CATEGORY:", cat, "(" + str(len(items)) + " findings)")
    print("=" * 80)
    for item in items:
        print()
        print("  File:", item["logfile"] + ", Line:", item["line_num"])
        print(" ", item["text"])
        if item["context"]:
            print("  Context:", item["context"])

print()
print()
print("=" * 80)
print("SUMMARY BY CATEGORY")
print("=" * 80)
for cat in sorted(categories.keys()):
    print(" ", cat + ":", len(categories[cat]))

print()
print()
print("=" * 80)
print("FRICTION ANALYSIS")
print("=" * 80)
cli_count = len(categories.get("CLI_STATUS_INVOCATION", []))
edit_count = len(categories.get("FRONTMATTER_STATUS_EDIT", []))
invalid_count = len(categories.get("FRONTMATTER_INVALID_TRANSITION", []))
error_count = len(categories.get("CLI_STATUS_ERROR", []))
rework_count = len(categories.get("REWORK_TRANSITION", []))
friction_count = len(categories.get("FRICTION_DISCUSSION", []))
write_count = len(categories.get("WRITE_WITH_STATUS", []))
print("  CLI openstation status invocations:", cli_count)
print("  Direct frontmatter status edits (Edit tool):", edit_count)
print("  Full file writes with status (Write tool):", write_count)
print("  Invalid transitions (direct edit):", invalid_count)
print("  CLI status errors:", error_count)
print("  Rework (review to in-progress):", rework_count)
print("  Agent friction discussions:", friction_count)
if edit_count > 0 and cli_count > 0:
    ratio = edit_count / (edit_count + cli_count)
    print()
    pct = str(int(ratio * 100))
    print("  Direct-edit ratio:", pct + "% of status changes bypass CLI")
elif edit_count > 0:
    print()
    print("  WARNING: ALL status changes done via direct frontmatter edits (no CLI usage)")
elif cli_count > 0:
    print()
    print("  All status changes done via CLI (no direct edits)")
else:
    print()
    print("  No status changes found in logs")

import argparse
import sys

from openstation import __version__ as VERSION
from openstation import core
from openstation import tasks
from openstation import run
from openstation import init


def main():
    fmt = argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="openstation",
        description="CLI for the Open Station task vault.",
        formatter_class=fmt,
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--version", action="version", version=f"openstation {VERSION}",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")

    # list
    list_p = sub.add_parser("list", help="List tasks", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation list                          # active tasks (ready + in-progress + review)
  openstation list --status all             # all tasks regardless of status
  openstation list --status ready --assignee researcher
  openstation list -q --status ready        # one task name per line (pipe-friendly)
  openstation list --json                   # JSON array of task objects
  openstation list --vim                    # open active tasks in vim
  openstation list --status ready --vim     # open only ready tasks in vim
  openstation list 0042                     # show task 0042 and its subtask tree""")
    list_p.add_argument("filter", nargs="?", default=None,
                        help="Task ID/slug or assignee name (auto-detected)")
    list_p.add_argument("--status", default=None,
                        help="Filter by status: backlog|ready|in-progress|review|done|failed|active|all "
                             "(default: active = ready + in-progress + review)")
    list_p.add_argument("--assignee", default="",
                        help="Filter by assignee (exact match)")
    list_output = list_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    list_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit output as a JSON array of task objects")
    list_output.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit one task name per line, no header (pipe-friendly)")
    list_output.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open matching task files in vim as a buffer list")
    list_p.add_argument("--type", default=None,
                        help="Filter by type: feature|research|spec|implementation|documentation")

    # agents (with sub-actions: list, show, dispatch)
    agents_p = sub.add_parser("agents", help="Agent operations", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation agents                        # list all agents (default)
  openstation agents list                   # same as bare 'agents'
  openstation agents list --json            # JSON array of agent objects
  openstation agents list --quiet           # one name per line (pipe-friendly)
  openstation agents show researcher        # print full agent spec
  openstation agents show researcher --json # frontmatter + body as JSON
  openstation agents show researcher --vim  # open in editor
  openstation agents dispatch researcher    # launch claude --agent researcher""")
    agents_sub = agents_p.add_subparsers(dest="agents_action")

    # agents list (also the default when no sub-action given)
    agents_list_p = agents_sub.add_parser("list", help="List agents")
    agents_list_output = agents_list_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agents_list_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit JSON array of agent objects")
    agents_list_output.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                        help="One agent name per line (pipe-friendly)")

    # agents show
    agents_show_p = agents_sub.add_parser("show", help="Show agent spec")
    agents_show_p.add_argument("name", help="Agent name")
    agents_show_output = agents_show_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agents_show_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit parsed frontmatter + body as JSON object")
    agents_show_output.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open the agent spec file in editor")

    # agents dispatch
    agents_dispatch_p = agents_sub.add_parser("dispatch", help="Launch agent")
    agents_dispatch_p.add_argument("name", help="Agent name")

    # show
    show_p = sub.add_parser("show", help="Show a task spec", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation show 0042                     # show by numeric ID
  openstation show 42                       # short ID (auto-padded)
  openstation show 0042-cli-improvements    # show by full name
  openstation show cli-improvements         # show by slug
  openstation show 0042 --json              # frontmatter + body as JSON
  openstation show 0042 --vim               # open in vim with markdown support""")
    show_p.add_argument("task", help="Task ID or slug (e.g. 0023, 23, or cli-feature-spec)")
    show_p.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit parsed frontmatter and body as a JSON object")
    show_p.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open the task file in vim (markdown plugins work)")

    # create
    create_p = sub.add_parser("create", help="Create a new task", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation create "add login page"
  openstation create "fix auth bug" --assignee developer --status ready
  openstation create "child task" --parent 0042""")
    create_p.add_argument("description", help="Task description (free text)")
    create_p.add_argument("--assignee", default="", help="Agent name to assign")
    create_p.add_argument("--owner", default="user", help="Who verifies (default: user)")
    create_p.add_argument("--status", default=None,
                          help="Initial status: backlog or ready (default: inherit from parent, else backlog)")
    create_p.add_argument("--type", default="feature",
                          help="Task type: feature|research|spec|implementation|documentation (default: feature)")
    create_p.add_argument("--parent", default="",
                          help="Parent task name (wikilink added automatically)")

    # status
    status_p = sub.add_parser("status", help="Change a task's status", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation status 0042 ready             # backlog → ready
  openstation status 42 in-progress         # short ID works
  openstation status cli-improvements review

valid transitions:
  backlog → ready → in-progress → review → done
                                  review → failed → in-progress""")
    status_p.add_argument("task", help="Task ID or slug")
    status_p.add_argument("new_status", help="Target status: backlog|ready|in-progress|review|done|failed")

    # run
    run_p = sub.add_parser("run", help="Launch an agent", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
Two modes of operation:

  By-agent:  openstation run <agent>          Execute agent's ready tasks
  By-task:   openstation run --task <id>      Execute a specific task

examples:
  openstation run researcher                # launch researcher agent
  openstation run researcher --dry-run      # show command without executing
  openstation run --task 0042 --dry-run     # preview task execution command
  openstation run --task 0042 --force       # skip status check
  openstation run --task 0042 --tier 1      # interactive mode
  openstation run --task 42 --dry-run --json  # structured JSON dry-run output""")
    run_p.add_argument("agent", nargs="?", default=None,
                       help="Agent name or task ID (auto-detected: numeric prefix → task)")
    run_p.add_argument("--task", default=None,
                       help="Task ID or slug (explicit, same as positional)")
    run_p.add_argument("--tier", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_TIER,
                       choices=[1, 2],
                       help="Execution tier: 1=interactive, 2=autonomous (default: 2)")
    run_p.add_argument("--budget", type=float, default=run.DEFAULT_BUDGET,
                       help="Max USD per invocation (tier 2, default: 5)")
    run_p.add_argument("--turns", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_TURNS,
                       help="Max turns per invocation (tier 2, default: 50)")
    run_p.add_argument("--max-tasks", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_MAX_TASKS,
                       help="Max subtasks to execute (by-task, default: 1)")
    run_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                       help="Skip task status checks")
    run_p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                       help="Print command without executing")
    run_p.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                       help="Suppress progress output (errors still shown)")
    run_p.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                       help="With --dry-run, emit structured JSON instead of shell command")

    # init
    init_p = sub.add_parser("init", help="Initialize Open Station in current directory",
                            formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation init                          # full init with all agents
  openstation init --agents researcher,author
  openstation init --no-agents
  openstation init --dry-run                # preview without writing""")
    agent_grp = init_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agent_grp.add_argument("--agents", default=None, metavar="NAMES",
                           help="Comma-separated agent names to install (default: all)")
    agent_grp.add_argument("--no-agents", action="store_true",
                           help="Skip installing example agent specs")
    init_p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                        help="Show what would be done without writing")
    init_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                        help="Overwrite user-owned files too")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.command:
        parser.print_help()
        return core.EXIT_USAGE

    # init operates on CWD, doesn't need find_root()
    if args.command == "init":
        return init.cmd_init(args) or 0

    root, prefix = core.find_root()
    if root is None:
        core.err("not in an Open Station project")
        core.err("  hint: run from a directory containing .openstation/")
        return core.EXIT_NO_PROJECT

    if args.command == "list":
        return tasks.cmd_list(args, root, prefix) or 0
    elif args.command == "agents":
        action = getattr(args, "agents_action", None)
        if action is None or action == "list":
            return run.cmd_agents_list(args, root, prefix) or 0
        elif action == "show":
            return run.cmd_agents_show(args, root, prefix)
        elif action == "dispatch":
            return run.cmd_agents_dispatch(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "show":
        return tasks.cmd_show(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "create":
        return tasks.cmd_create(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "status":
        return tasks.cmd_status(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "run":
        return run.cmd_run(args, root, prefix)
    return core.EXIT_OK

import argparse
import sys

from openstation import __version__ as VERSION
from openstation import core
from openstation import tasks
from openstation import run
from openstation import init


def main():
    fmt = argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="openstation",
        description="CLI for the Open Station task vault.",
        formatter_class=fmt,
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--version", action="version", version=f"openstation {VERSION}",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")

    # list
    list_p = sub.add_parser("list", help="List tasks", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation list                          # active tasks (ready + in-progress + review)
  openstation list --status all             # all tasks regardless of status
  openstation list --status ready --assignee researcher
  openstation list -q --status ready        # one task name per line (pipe-friendly)
  openstation list --json                   # JSON array of task objects
  openstation list --vim                    # open active tasks in vim
  openstation list --status ready --vim     # open only ready tasks in vim
  openstation list 0042                     # show task 0042 and its subtask tree""")
    list_p.add_argument("filter", nargs="?", default=None,
                        help="Task ID/slug or assignee name (auto-detected)")
    list_p.add_argument("--status", default=None,
                        help="Filter by status: backlog|ready|in-progress|review|done|failed|active|all "
                             "(default: active = ready + in-progress + review)")
    list_p.add_argument("--assignee", default="",
                        help="Filter by assignee (exact match)")
    list_output = list_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    list_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit output as a JSON array of task objects")
    list_output.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit one task name per line, no header (pipe-friendly)")
    list_output.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open matching task files in vim as a buffer list")
    list_p.add_argument("--type", default=None,
                        help="Filter by type: feature|research|spec|implementation|documentation")

    # agents (with sub-actions: list, show, dispatch)
    agents_p = sub.add_parser("agents", aliases=["ag"], help="Agent operations", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation agents                        # list all agents (default)
  openstation agents list                   # same as bare 'agents'
  openstation agents list --json            # JSON array of agent objects
  openstation agents list --quiet           # one name per line (pipe-friendly)
  openstation agents show researcher        # print full agent spec
  openstation agents show researcher --json # frontmatter + body as JSON
  openstation agents show researcher --vim  # open in editor
  openstation agents dispatch researcher    # launch claude --agent researcher""")
    agents_sub = agents_p.add_subparsers(dest="agents_action")

    # agents list (also the default when no sub-action given)
    agents_list_p = agents_sub.add_parser("list", help="List agents")
    agents_list_output = agents_list_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agents_list_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit JSON array of agent objects")
    agents_list_output.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                        help="One agent name per line (pipe-friendly)")

    # agents show
    agents_show_p = agents_sub.add_parser("show", help="Show agent spec")
    agents_show_p.add_argument("name", help="Agent name")
    agents_show_output = agents_show_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agents_show_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit parsed frontmatter + body as JSON object")
    agents_show_output.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open the agent spec file in editor")

    # agents dispatch
    agents_dispatch_p = agents_sub.add_parser("dispatch", help="Launch agent")
    agents_dispatch_p.add_argument("name", help="Agent name")

    # show
    show_p = sub.add_parser("show", help="Show a task spec", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation show 0042                     # show by numeric ID
  openstation show 42                       # short ID (auto-padded)
  openstation show 0042-cli-improvements    # show by full name
  openstation show cli-improvements         # show by slug
  openstation show 0042 --json              # frontmatter + body as JSON
  openstation show 0042 --vim               # open in vim with markdown support""")
    show_p.add_argument("task", help="Task ID or slug (e.g. 0023, 23, or cli-feature-spec)")
    show_p.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit parsed frontmatter and body as a JSON object")
    show_p.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open the task file in vim (markdown plugins work)")

    # create
    create_p = sub.add_parser("create", help="Create a new task", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation create "add login page"
  openstation create "fix auth bug" --assignee developer --status ready
  openstation create "child task" --parent 0042""")
    create_p.add_argument("description", help="Task description (free text)")
    create_p.add_argument("--assignee", default="", help="Agent name to assign")
    create_p.add_argument("--owner", default="user", help="Who verifies (default: user)")
    create_p.add_argument("--status", default=None,
                          help="Initial status: backlog or ready (default: inherit from parent, else backlog)")
    create_p.add_argument("--type", default="feature",
                          help="Task type: feature|research|spec|implementation|documentation (default: feature)")
    create_p.add_argument("--parent", default="",
                          help="Parent task name (wikilink added automatically)")

    # status
    status_p = sub.add_parser("status", help="Change a task's status", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation status 0042 ready             # backlog → ready
  openstation status 42 in-progress         # short ID works
  openstation status cli-improvements review

valid transitions:
  backlog → ready → in-progress → review → done
                                  review → failed → in-progress""")
    status_p.add_argument("task", help="Task ID or slug")
    status_p.add_argument("new_status", help="Target status: backlog|ready|in-progress|review|done|failed")

    # run
    run_p = sub.add_parser("run", help="Launch an agent", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
Two modes of operation:

  By-agent:  openstation run <agent>          Execute agent's ready tasks
  By-task:   openstation run --task <id>      Execute a specific task

examples:
  openstation run researcher                # launch researcher agent
  openstation run researcher --dry-run      # show command without executing
  openstation run --task 0042 --dry-run     # preview task execution command
  openstation run --task 0042 --force       # skip status check
  openstation run --task 0042 --tier 1      # interactive mode
  openstation run --task 42 --dry-run --json  # structured JSON dry-run output""")
    run_p.add_argument("agent", nargs="?", default=None,
                       help="Agent name or task ID (auto-detected: numeric prefix → task)")
    run_p.add_argument("--task", default=None,
                       help="Task ID or slug (explicit, same as positional)")
    run_p.add_argument("--tier", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_TIER,
                       choices=[1, 2],
                       help="Execution tier: 1=interactive, 2=autonomous (default: 2)")
    run_p.add_argument("--budget", type=float, default=run.DEFAULT_BUDGET,
                       help="Max USD per invocation (tier 2, default: 5)")
    run_p.add_argument("--turns", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_TURNS,
                       help="Max turns per invocation (tier 2, default: 50)")
    run_p.add_argument("--max-tasks", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_MAX_TASKS,
                       help="Max subtasks to execute (by-task, default: 1)")
    run_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                       help="Skip task status checks")
    run_p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                       help="Print command without executing")
    run_p.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                       help="Suppress progress output (errors still shown)")
    run_p.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                       help="With --dry-run, emit structured JSON instead of shell command")

    # init
    init_p = sub.add_parser("init", help="Initialize Open Station in current directory",
                            formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation init                          # full init with all agents
  openstation init --agents researcher,author
  openstation init --no-agents
  openstation init --dry-run                # preview without writing""")
    agent_grp = init_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agent_grp.add_argument("--agents", default=None, metavar="NAMES",
                           help="Comma-separated agent names to install (default: all)")
    agent_grp.add_argument("--no-agents", action="store_true",
                           help="Skip installing example agent specs")
    init_p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                        help="Show what would be done without writing")
    init_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                        help="Overwrite user-owned files too")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.command:
        parser.print_help()
        return core.EXIT_USAGE

    # init operates on CWD, doesn't need find_root()
    if args.command == "init":
        return init.cmd_init(args) or 0

    root, prefix = core.find_root()
    if root is None:
        core.err("not in an Open Station project")
        core.err("  hint: run from a directory containing .openstation/")
        return core.EXIT_NO_PROJECT

    if args.command == "list":
        return tasks.cmd_list(args, root, prefix) or 0
    elif args.command == "agents":
        action = getattr(args, "agents_action", None)
        if action is None or action == "list":
            return run.cmd_agents_list(args, root, prefix) or 0
        elif action == "show":
            return run.cmd_agents_show(args, root, prefix)
        elif action == "dispatch":
            return run.cmd_agents_dispatch(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "show":
        return tasks.cmd_show(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "create":
        return tasks.cmd_create(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "status":
        return tasks.cmd_status(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "run":
        return run.cmd_run(args, root, prefix)
    return core.EXIT_OK

                        help="Show what would be done without writing")
    init_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                        help="Overwrite user-owned files too")","originalFile":""""Entry point: argparse definition, subcommand routing, and main()."""

import argparse
import sys

from openstation import __version__ as VERSION
from openstation import core
from openstation import tasks
from openstation import run
from openstation import init


def main():
    fmt = argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="openstation",
        description="CLI for the Open Station task vault.",
        formatter_class=fmt,
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--version", action="version", version=f"openstation {VERSION}",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")

    # list
    list_p = sub.add_parser("list", help="List tasks", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation list                          # active tasks (ready + in-progress + review)
  openstation list --status all             # all tasks regardless of status
  openstation list --status ready --assignee researcher
  openstation list -q --status ready        # one task name per line (pipe-friendly)
  openstation list --json                   # JSON array of task objects
  openstation list --vim                    # open active tasks in vim
  openstation list --status ready --vim     # open only ready tasks in vim
  openstation list 0042                     # show task 0042 and its subtask tree""")
    list_p.add_argument("filter", nargs="?", default=None,
                        help="Task ID/slug or assignee name (auto-detected)")
    list_p.add_argument("--status", default=None,
                        help="Filter by status: backlog|ready|in-progress|review|done|failed|active|all "
                             "(default: active = ready + in-progress + review)")
    list_p.add_argument("--assignee", default="",
                        help="Filter by assignee (exact match)")
    list_output = list_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    list_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit output as a JSON array of task objects")
    list_output.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit one task name per line, no header (pipe-friendly)")
    list_output.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open matching task files in vim as a buffer list")
    list_p.add_argument("--type", default=None,
                        help="Filter by type: feature|research|spec|implementation|documentation")

    # agents (with sub-actions: list, show, dispatch)
    agents_p = sub.add_parser("agents", aliases=["ag"], help="Agent operations", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation agents                        # list all agents (default)
  openstation agents list                   # same as bare 'agents'
  openstation agents list --json            # JSON array of agent objects
  openstation agents list --quiet           # one name per line (pipe-friendly)
  openstation agents show researcher        # print full agent spec
  openstation agents show researcher --json # frontmatter + body as JSON
  openstation agents show researcher --vim  # open in editor
  openstation agents dispatch researcher    # launch claude --agent researcher""")
    agents_sub = agents_p.add_subparsers(dest="agents_action")

    # agents list (also the default when no sub-action given)
    agents_list_p = agents_sub.add_parser("list", help="List agents")
    agents_list_output = agents_list_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agents_list_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit JSON array of agent objects")
    agents_list_output.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                        help="One agent name per line (pipe-friendly)")

    # agents show
    agents_show_p = agents_sub.add_parser("show", help="Show agent spec")
    agents_show_p.add_argument("name", help="Agent name")
    agents_show_output = agents_show_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agents_show_output.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit parsed frontmatter + body as JSON object")
    agents_show_output.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open the agent spec file in editor")

    # agents dispatch
    agents_dispatch_p = agents_sub.add_parser("dispatch", help="Launch agent")
    agents_dispatch_p.add_argument("name", help="Agent name")
    agents_dispatch_p.add_argument("--dangerously-skip-permissions",
                                   action="store_true", default=False,
                                   help="Pass --dangerously-skip-permissions to claude")

    # show
    show_p = sub.add_parser("show", help="Show a task spec", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation show 0042                     # show by numeric ID
  openstation show 42                       # short ID (auto-padded)
  openstation show 0042-cli-improvements    # show by full name
  openstation show cli-improvements         # show by slug
  openstation show 0042 --json              # frontmatter + body as JSON
  openstation show 0042 --vim               # open in vim with markdown support""")
    show_p.add_argument("task", help="Task ID or slug (e.g. 0023, 23, or cli-feature-spec)")
    show_p.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                        help="Emit parsed frontmatter and body as a JSON object")
    show_p.add_argument("-v", "--vim", action="store_true",
                        help="Open the task file in vim (markdown plugins work)")

    # create
    create_p = sub.add_parser("create", help="Create a new task", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation create "add login page"
  openstation create "fix auth bug" --assignee developer --status ready
  openstation create "child task" --parent 0042""")
    create_p.add_argument("description", help="Task description (free text)")
    create_p.add_argument("--assignee", default="", help="Agent name to assign")
    create_p.add_argument("--owner", default="user", help="Who verifies (default: user)")
    create_p.add_argument("--status", default=None,
                          help="Initial status: backlog or ready (default: inherit from parent, else backlog)")
    create_p.add_argument("--type", default="feature",
                          help="Task type: feature|research|spec|implementation|documentation (default: feature)")
    create_p.add_argument("--parent", default="",
                          help="Parent task name (wikilink added automatically)")

    # status
    status_p = sub.add_parser("status", help="Change a task's status", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation status 0042 ready             # backlog → ready
  openstation status 42 in-progress         # short ID works
  openstation status cli-improvements review

valid transitions:
  backlog → ready → in-progress → review → done
                                  review → failed → in-progress""")
    status_p.add_argument("task", help="Task ID or slug")
    status_p.add_argument("new_status", help="Target status: backlog|ready|in-progress|review|done|failed")

    # run
    run_p = sub.add_parser("run", help="Launch an agent", formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
Two modes of operation:

  By-agent:  openstation run <agent>          Execute agent's ready tasks
  By-task:   openstation run --task <id>      Execute a specific task

examples:
  openstation run researcher                # launch researcher agent
  openstation run researcher --dry-run      # show command without executing
  openstation run --task 0042 --dry-run     # preview task execution command
  openstation run --task 0042 --force       # skip status check
  openstation run --task 0042 --tier 1      # interactive mode
  openstation run --task 42 --dry-run --json  # structured JSON dry-run output""")
    run_p.add_argument("agent", nargs="?", default=None,
                       help="Agent name or task ID (auto-detected: numeric prefix → task)")
    run_p.add_argument("--task", default=None,
                       help="Task ID or slug (explicit, same as positional)")
    run_p.add_argument("--tier", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_TIER,
                       choices=[1, 2],
                       help="Execution tier: 1=interactive, 2=autonomous (default: 2)")
    run_p.add_argument("--budget", type=float, default=run.DEFAULT_BUDGET,
                       help="Max USD per invocation (tier 2, default: 5)")
    run_p.add_argument("--turns", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_TURNS,
                       help="Max turns per invocation (tier 2, default: 50)")
    run_p.add_argument("--max-tasks", type=int, default=run.DEFAULT_MAX_TASKS,
                       help="Max subtasks to execute (by-task, default: 1)")
    run_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                       help="Skip task status checks")
    run_p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                       help="Print command without executing")
    run_p.add_argument("-q", "--quiet", action="store_true",
                       help="Suppress progress output (errors still shown)")
    run_p.add_argument("-j", "--json", action="store_true",
                       help="With --dry-run, emit structured JSON instead of shell command")

    # init
    init_p = sub.add_parser("init", help="Initialize Open Station in current directory",
                            formatter_class=fmt, epilog="""\
examples:
  openstation init                          # full init with all agents
  openstation init --agents researcher,author
  openstation init --no-agents
  openstation init --user                   # install to ~/.claude/ instead
  openstation init --dry-run                # preview without writing""")
    agent_grp = init_p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    agent_grp.add_argument("--agents", default=None, metavar="NAMES",
                           help="Comma-separated agent names to install (default: all)")
    agent_grp.add_argument("--no-agents", action="store_true",
                           help="Skip installing example agent specs")
    init_p.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true",
                        help="Show what would be done without writing")
    init_p.add_argument("--force", action="store_true",
                        help="Overwrite user-owned files too")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.command:
        parser.print_help()
        return core.EXIT_USAGE

    # init operates on CWD, doesn't need find_root()
    if args.command == "init":
        return init.cmd_init(args) or 0

    root, prefix = core.find_root()
    if root is None:
        core.err("not in an Open Station project")
        core.err("  hint: run from a directory containing .openstation/")
        return core.EXIT_NO_PROJECT

    if args.command == "list":
        return tasks.cmd_list(args, root, prefix) or 0
    elif args.command in ("agents", "ag"):
        action = getattr(args, "agents_action", None)
        if action is None or action == "list":
            return run.cmd_agents_list(args, root, prefix) or 0
        elif action == "show":
            return run.cmd_agents_show(args, root, prefix)
        elif action == "dispatch":
            return run.cmd_agents_dispatch(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "show":
        return tasks.cmd_show(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "create":
        return tasks.cmd_create(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "status":
        return tasks.cmd_status(args, root, prefix)
    elif args.command == "run":
        return run.cmd_run(args, root, prefix)
    return core.EXIT_OK

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Parse JSONL agent session logs to find lifecycle transition friction.

Looks for:
1. "openstation status" CLI invocations and their results
2. Edit tool calls that modify frontmatter "status:" fields
3. Assistant text discussing transition difficulties or workarounds
4. Failed/invalid transitions
5. review -> in-progress (rework) patterns
"""

import json
import os
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

LOGS_DIR = Path("/Users/leonid/workspace/open-station/artifacts/logs")

# Allowed transitions per lifecycle.md
ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS = {
    ("backlog", "ready"),
    ("ready", "in-progress"),
    ("ready", "backlog"),
    ("in-progress", "review"),
    ("review", "done"),
    ("review", "failed"),
    ("failed", "in-progress"),
}

STATUSES = {"backlog", "ready", "in-progress", "review", "done", "failed"}

# Keywords suggesting friction
FRICTION_KEYWORDS = [
    r"cannot\s+(?:change|transition|move|set)\s+status",
    r"(?:invalid|illegal|not\s+allowed|disallowed)\s+(?:transition|status)",
    r"transition.*(?:error|fail|invalid|not\s+allowed)",
    r"status.*(?:error|fail|invalid|not\s+allowed)",
    r"workaround",
    r"directly\s+edit(?:ing)?\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:frontmatter|yaml|status)",
    r"manual(?:ly)?\s+(?:edit|change|update|set)\s+(?:the\s+)?status",
    r"can(?:'t|not)\s+(?:go|move|transition)\s+(?:from|back)",
    r"no\s+(?:direct\s+)?(?:transition|path)\s+(?:from|between)",
    r"(?:missing|need|want)\s+(?:a\s+)?transition",
    r"(?:skip|bypass)(?:ing)?\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:transition|status|lifecycle)",
    r"review\s+(?:back\s+)?to\s+in-progress",
    r"rework",
    r"unable\s+to\s+(?:change|transition|move|set)",
]

findings = []

def add_finding(category, logfile, line_num, text, context=""):
    findings.append({
        "category": category,
        "logfile": logfile,
        "line_num": line_num,
        "text": text[:500],
        "context": context[:300] if context else "",
    })


def extract_tool_use_content(content_list):
    """Extract tool_use blocks from assistant message content."""
    results = []
    if not isinstance(content_list, list):
        return results
    for block in content_list:
        if isinstance(block, dict):
            if block.get("type") == "tool_use":
                results.append(block)
            elif block.get("type") == "text":
                results.append(block)
    return results


def extract_text_content(content_list):
    """Extract text from assistant message content."""
    texts = []
    if not isinstance(content_list, list):
        return ""
    for block in content_list:
        if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "text":
            texts.append(block.get("text", ""))
    return "\n".join(texts)


def analyze_file(filepath):
    logfile = filepath.name
    lines = []

    with open(filepath, "r") as f:
        for i, raw_line in enumerate(f, 1):
            raw_line = raw_line.strip()
            if not raw_line:
                continue
            try:
                obj = json.loads(raw_line)
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                continue
            lines.append((i, obj))

    # Build a map of tool_use_id -> (line_num, tool_name, command/input)
    tool_uses = {}

    for line_num, obj in lines:
        msg_type = obj.get("type")

        # --- Assistant messages ---
        if msg_type == "assistant":
            message = obj.get("message", {})
            content = message.get("content", [])

            # Check text blocks for friction keywords
            full_text = extract_text_content(content)
            if full_text:
                for pattern in FRICTION_KEYWORDS:
                    if re.search(pattern, full_text, re.IGNORECASE):
                        # Get a snippet around the match
                        match = re.search(pattern, full_text, re.IGNORECASE)
                        start = max(0, match.start() - 100)
                        end = min(len(full_text), match.end() + 200)
                        snippet = full_text[start:end]
                        add_finding(
                            "FRICTION_DISCUSSION",
                            logfile, line_num,
                            f"Agent discusses transition friction: ...{snippet}..."
                        )
                        break  # one finding per text block

            # Check tool_use blocks
            for block in extract_tool_use_content(content):
                if block.get("type") != "tool_use":
                    continue

                tool_name = block.get("name", "")
                tool_id = block.get("id", "")
                tool_input = block.get("input", {})

                # --- "openstation status" CLI calls ---
                if tool_name == "Bash":
                    cmd = tool_input.get("command", "")
                    if "openstation status" in cmd or "openstation status" in cmd.replace("  ", " "):
                        tool_uses[tool_id] = (line_num, "openstation_status", cmd)
                        add_finding(
                            "CLI_STATUS_INVOCATION",
                            logfile, line_num,
                            f"openstation status command: {cmd}"
                        )
                        # Try to extract from/to status
                        m = re.search(r'openstation\s+status\s+(\S+)\s+(\S+)', cmd)
                        if m:
                            task_ref = m.group(1)
                            new_status = m.group(2).strip("'\"")
                            add_finding(
                                "CLI_STATUS_TRANSITION",
                                logfile, line_num,
                                f"Transition attempt: task={task_ref} -> {new_status}",
                                context=cmd
                            )

                # --- Edit tool calls changing status ---
                if tool_name == "Edit":
                    old_str = tool_input.get("old_string", "")
                    new_str = tool_input.get("new_string", "")
                    file_path = tool_input.get("file_path", "")

                    # Check if editing status in frontmatter
                    old_status_match = re.search(r'status:\s*(\S+)', old_str)
                    new_status_match = re.search(r'status:\s*(\S+)', new_str)

                    if old_status_match and new_status_match:
                        old_s = old_status_match.group(1).strip("'\"")
                        new_s = new_status_match.group(1).strip("'\"")
                        if old_s != new_s:
                            is_allowed = (old_s, new_s) in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS
                            category = "FRONTMATTER_STATUS_EDIT"
                            if not is_allowed:
                                category = "FRONTMATTER_INVALID_TRANSITION"
                            add_finding(
                                category,
                                logfile, line_num,
                                f"Direct frontmatter edit: {old_s} -> {new_s} (allowed={is_allowed}) in {file_path}",
                                context=f"old: {old_str[:150]}\nnew: {new_str[:150]}"
                            )

                            # Special case: review -> in-progress (rework)
                            if old_s == "review" and new_s == "in-progress":
                                add_finding(
                                    "REWORK_TRANSITION",
                                    logfile, line_num,
                                    f"Rework: review -> in-progress via direct edit in {file_path}"
                                )
                    elif new_status_match and not old_status_match:
                        # Adding status where there wasn't one, or changing context
                        pass

                # --- Write tool calls (full file rewrites with status changes) ---
                if tool_name == "Write":
                    content_str = tool_input.get("content", "")
                    file_path = tool_input.get("file_path", "")
                    if "status:" in content_str and ("artifacts/tasks/" in file_path or ".openstation/" in file_path):
                        status_match = re.search(r'status:\s*(\S+)', content_str)
                        if status_match:
                            add_finding(
                                "WRITE_WITH_STATUS",
                                logfile, line_num,
                                f"Full file write with status={status_match.group(1)} to {file_path}"
                            )

        # --- Tool results ---
        if msg_type == "result":
            tool_use_id = obj.get("tool_use_id", "")
            result_content = obj.get("result", "")

            # Handle various result formats
            if isinstance(result_content, list):
                result_text = " ".join(
                    str(r.get("text", "")) if isinstance(r, dict) else str(r)
                    for r in result_content
                )
            elif isinstance(result_content, dict):
                result_text = str(result_content.get("text", result_content.get("output", str(result_content))))
            else:
                result_text = str(result_content)

            # Check if this is a result for an openstation status command
            if tool_use_id in tool_uses:
                orig_line, tool_type, orig_cmd = tool_uses[tool_use_id]
                if tool_type == "openstation_status":
                    # Check for errors
                    if any(kw in result_text.lower() for kw in ["error", "invalid", "fail", "not allowed", "cannot"]):
                        add_finding(
                            "CLI_STATUS_ERROR",
                            logfile, line_num,
                            f"Status command failed: {result_text[:400]}",
                            context=f"Original command (line {orig_line}): {orig_cmd}"
                        )
                    else:
                        add_finding(
                            "CLI_STATUS_SUCCESS",
                            logfile, line_num,
                            f"Status command result: {result_text[:400]}",
                            context=f"Original command (line {orig_line}): {orig_cmd}"
                        )

            # Also check any tool result for transition error messages
            if re.search(r'(?:invalid|illegal|not\s+allowed)\s+transition', result_text, re.IGNORECASE):
                add_finding(
                    "TRANSITION_ERROR_IN_RESULT",
                    logfile, line_num,
                    f"Transition error in tool result: {result_text[:400]}"
                )


def main():
    log_files = sorted(LOGS_DIR.glob("*.jsonl"))

    if not log_files:
        print("No JSONL files found!")
        sys.exit(1)

    print(f"Analyzing {len(log_files)} log files...\n")

    for lf in log_files:
        print(f"  Processing: {lf.name}")
        analyze_file(lf)

    print(f"\n{'='*80}")
    print(f"TOTAL FINDINGS: {len(findings)}")
    print(f"{'='*80}\n")

    # Group by category
    categories = {}
    for f in findings:
        cat = f["category"]
        if cat not in categories:
            categories[cat] = []
        categories[cat].append(f)

    for cat in sorted(categories.keys()):
        items = categories[cat]
        print(f"\n{'='*80}")
        print(f"CATEGORY: {cat} ({len(items)} findings)")
        print(f"{'='*80}")
        for item in items:
            print(f"\n  File: {item['logfile']}, Line: {item['line_num']}")
            print(f"  {item['text']}")
            if item['context']:
                print(f"  Context: {item['context']}")

    # Summary
    print(f"\n\n{'='*80}")
    print("SUMMARY BY CATEGORY")
    print(f"{'='*80}")
    for cat in sorted(categories.keys()):
        print(f"  {cat}: {len(categories[cat])}")

    # Specific friction analysis
    print(f"\n\n{'='*80}")
    print("FRICTION ANALYSIS")
    print(f"{'='*80}")

    # Count direct edits vs CLI usage
    cli_count = len(categories.get("CLI_STATUS_INVOCATION", []))
    edit_count = len(categories.get("FRONTMATTER_STATUS_EDIT", []))
    invalid_count = len(categories.get("FRONTMATTER_INVALID_TRANSITION", []))
    error_count = len(categories.get("CLI_STATUS_ERROR", []))
    rework_count = len(categories.get("REWORK_TRANSITION", []))
    friction_count = len(categories.get("FRICTION_DISCUSSION", []))

    print(f"  CLI 'openstation status' invocations: {cli_count}")
    print(f"  Direct frontmatter status edits: {edit_count}")
    print(f"  Invalid transitions (direct edit): {invalid_count}")
    print(f"  CLI status errors: {error_count}")
    print(f"  Rework (review->in-progress): {rework_count}")
    print(f"  Agent friction discussions: {friction_count}")

    if edit_count > 0 and cli_count > 0:
        ratio = edit_count / (edit_count + cli_count)
        print(f"\n  Direct-edit ratio: {ratio:.0%} of status changes bypass CLI")
    elif edit_count > 0:
        print(f"\n  WARNING: ALL status changes done via direct frontmatter edits (no CLI usage)")
    elif cli_count > 0:
        print(f"\n  All status changes done via CLI (no direct edits)")
    else:
        print(f"\n  No status changes found in logs")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
PYEOF

    r"directly\s+edit(?:ing)?\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:frontmatter|yaml|status)",
    r"manual(?:ly)?\s+(?:edit|change|update|set)\s+(?:the\s+)?status",
    r"no\s+(?:direct\s+)?(?:transition|path)\s+(?:from|between)",
    r"(?:missing|need|want)\s+(?:a\s+)?transition",
    r"(?:skip|bypass)(?:ing)?\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:transition|status|lifecycle)",
    r"review\s+(?:back\s+)?to\s+in.progress",
    r"rework",
    r"unable\s+to\s+(?:change|transition|move|set)",
]

findings = []

def add_finding(category, logfile, line_num, text, context=""):
    findings.append({
        "category": category,
        "logfile": logfile,
        "line_num": line_num,
        "text": text[:500],
        "context": context[:300] if context else "",
    })

def extract_text_content(content_list):
    texts = []
    if not isinstance(content_list, list):
        return ""
    for block in content_list:
        if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "text":
            texts.append(block.get("text", ""))
    return "\n".join(texts)

def analyze_file(filepath):
    logfile = filepath.name
    lines = []
    with open(filepath, "r") as f:
        for i, raw_line in enumerate(f, 1):
            raw_line = raw_line.strip()
            if not raw_line:
                continue
            try:
                obj = json.loads(raw_line)
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                continue
            lines.append((i, obj))

    tool_uses = {}

    for line_num, obj in lines:
        msg_type = obj.get("type")

        if msg_type == "assistant":
            message = obj.get("message", {})
            content = message.get("content", [])

            full_text = extract_text_content(content)
            if full_text:
                for pattern in FRICTION_KEYWORDS:
                    if re.search(pattern, full_text, re.IGNORECASE):
                        match = re.search(pattern, full_text, re.IGNORECASE)
                        start = max(0, match.start() - 100)
                        end = min(len(full_text), match.end() + 200)
                        snippet = full_text[start:end]
                        add_finding("FRICTION_DISCUSSION", logfile, line_num,
                            "Agent discusses transition friction: ..." + snippet + "...")
                        break

            if not isinstance(content, list):
                continue
            for block in content:
                if not isinstance(block, dict) or block.get("type") != "tool_use":
                    continue

                tool_name = block.get("name", "")
                tool_id = block.get("id", "")
                tool_input = block.get("input", {})

                if tool_name == "Bash":
                    cmd = tool_input.get("command", "")
                    if "openstation status" in cmd:
                        tool_uses[tool_id] = (line_num, "openstation_status", cmd)
                        add_finding("CLI_STATUS_INVOCATION", logfile, line_num,
                            "openstation status command: " + cmd)
                        m = re.search(r"openstation\s+status\s+(\S+)\s+(\S+)", cmd)
                        if m:
                            task_ref = m.group(1)
                            new_status = m.group(2).strip("'\"")
                            add_finding("CLI_STATUS_TRANSITION", logfile, line_num,
                                "Transition attempt: task=" + task_ref + " -> " + new_status,
                                context=cmd)

                if tool_name == "Edit":
                    old_str = tool_input.get("old_string", "")
                    new_str = tool_input.get("new_string", "")
                    file_path = tool_input.get("file_path", "")

                    old_status_match = re.search(r"status:\s*(\S+)", old_str)
                    new_status_match = re.search(r"status:\s*(\S+)", new_str)

                    if old_status_match and new_status_match:
                        old_s = old_status_match.group(1).strip("'\"")
                        new_s = new_status_match.group(1).strip("'\"")
                        if old_s != new_s:
                            is_allowed = (old_s, new_s) in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS
                            category = "FRONTMATTER_STATUS_EDIT"
                            if not is_allowed:
                                category = "FRONTMATTER_INVALID_TRANSITION"
                            add_finding(category, logfile, line_num,
                                "Direct frontmatter edit: " + old_s + " -> " + new_s + " (allowed=" + str(is_allowed) + ") in " + file_path,
                                context="old: " + old_str[:150] + " | new: " + new_str[:150])
                            if old_s == "review" and new_s == "in-progress":
                                add_finding("REWORK_TRANSITION", logfile, line_num,
                                    "Rework: review -> in-progress via direct edit in " + file_path)

                if tool_name == "Write":
                    content_str = tool_input.get("content", "")
                    file_path = tool_input.get("file_path", "")
                    if "status:" in content_str and ("artifacts/tasks/" in file_path or ".openstation/" in file_path):
                        status_match = re.search(r"status:\s*(\S+)", content_str)
                        if status_match:
                            add_finding("WRITE_WITH_STATUS", logfile, line_num,
                                "Full file write with status=" + status_match.group(1) + " to " + file_path)

        if msg_type == "result":
            tool_use_id = obj.get("tool_use_id", "")
            result_content = obj.get("result", "")
            if isinstance(result_content, list):
                result_text = " ".join(
                    str(r.get("text", "")) if isinstance(r, dict) else str(r)
                    for r in result_content
                )
            elif isinstance(result_content, dict):
                result_text = str(result_content.get("text", result_content.get("output", str(result_content))))
            else:
                result_text = str(result_content)

            if tool_use_id in tool_uses:
                orig_line, tool_type, orig_cmd = tool_uses[tool_use_id]
                if tool_type == "openstation_status":
                    if any(kw in result_text.lower() for kw in ["error", "invalid", "fail", "not allowed", "cannot"]):
                        add_finding("CLI_STATUS_ERROR", logfile, line_num,
                            "Status command failed: " + result_text[:400],
                            context="Original command (line " + str(orig_line) + "): " + orig_cmd)
                    else:
                        add_finding("CLI_STATUS_SUCCESS", logfile, line_num,
                            "Status command result: " + result_text[:400],
                            context="Original command (line " + str(orig_line) + "): " + orig_cmd)

            if re.search(r"(?:invalid|illegal|not\s+allowed)\s+transition", result_text, re.IGNORECASE):
                add_finding("TRANSITION_ERROR_IN_RESULT", logfile, line_num,
                    "Transition error in tool result: " + result_text[:400])


log_files = sorted(LOGS_DIR.glob("*.jsonl"))
print("Analyzing", len(log_files), "log files...")
print()
for lf in log_files:
    print("  Processing:", lf.name)
    analyze_file(lf)

print()
print("=" * 80)
print("TOTAL FINDINGS:", len(findings))
print("=" * 80)
print()

categories = {}
for f in findings:
    cat = f["category"]
    if cat not in categories:
        categories[cat] = []
    categories[cat].append(f)

for cat in sorted(categories.keys()):
    items = categories[cat]
    print()
    print("=" * 80)
    print("CATEGORY:", cat, "(" + str(len(items)) + " findings)")
    print("=" * 80)
    for item in items:
        print()
        print("  File:", item["logfile"] + ", Line:", item["line_num"])
        print(" ", item["text"])
        if item["context"]:
            print("  Context:", item["context"])

print()
print()
print("=" * 80)
print("SUMMARY BY CATEGORY")
print("=" * 80)
for cat in sorted(categories.keys()):
    print(" ", cat + ":", len(categories[cat]))

print()
print()
print("=" * 80)
print("FRICTION ANALYSIS")
print("=" * 80)
cli_count = len(categories.get("CLI_STATUS_INVOCATION", []))
edit_count = len(categories.get("FRONTMATTER_STATUS_EDIT", []))
invalid_count = len(categories.get("FRONTMATTER_INVALID_TRANSITION", []))
error_count = len(categories.get("CLI_STATUS_ERROR", []))
rework_count = len(categories.get("REWORK_TRANSITION", []))
friction_count = len(categories.get("FRICTION_DISCUSSION", []))
write_count = len(categories.get("WRITE_WITH_STATUS", []))
print("  CLI openstation status invocations:", cli_count)
print("  Direct frontmatter status edits (Edit tool):", edit_count)
print("  Full file writes with status (Write tool):", write_count)
print("  Invalid transitions (direct edit):", invalid_count)
print("  CLI status errors:", error_count)
print("  Rework (review to in-progress):", rework_count)
print("  Agent friction discussions:", friction_count)
if edit_count > 0 and cli_count > 0:
    ratio = edit_count / (edit_count + cli_count)
    print()
    pct = str(int(ratio * 100))
    print("  Direct-edit ratio:", pct + "% of status changes bypass CLI")
elif edit_count > 0:
    print()
    print("  WARNING: ALL status changes done via direct frontmatter edits (no CLI usage)")
elif cli_count > 0:
    print()
    print("  All status changes done via CLI (no direct edits)")
else:
    print()
    print("  No status changes found in logs")
